segments.text: Don't make yourselves idols anywhere in your land and bow down to worship them, whether they are images or sacred pillars or stone sculptures. For I am the Lord your God.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Keep my Sabbaths and show respect for my sanctuary. I am the Lord.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If you follow my rules and keep my regulations,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I will make sure it rains at the right time so that the ground will grow good crops and the fruit trees will be productive.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Your time of threshing will last right up to the grape harvest, and the grape harvest until the time you have to sow again. You will have more than enough to eat and you will live in safety in your land.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I will make sure your land is at peace. You will be able to sleep without being afraid of anything. I will get rid of dangerous animals from the land, and you will not suffer from any violent enemy attacks. [^49]
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You will chase your enemies, and kill them with the sword.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Five of you will kill one hundred, and one hundred of you will kill ten thousand. You will destroy your enemies.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I will come to help you, so you will reproduce and increase in numbers, and I will confirm my agreement with you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You'll still be eating your old stock of grain when you need to get rid of it so you can store the new grain.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I will come and live with you—I certainly won't reject you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I shall always be right there with you. I will be your God, and you will be my people.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I am the Lord your God, who led you out of Egypt so you wouldn't have to be slaves to the Egyptians any longer. I smashed the yoke that kept you bent down and helped you to st and up straight.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: But if you don't pay attention to me and do what I tell you;
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: if you reject my laws, hate my regulations, and refuse to follow my commandments, and consequently break my agreement,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: then this is what I am going to do to you: I will make you panic, and suffer from diseases like tuberculosis and fever that make you blind and drain your life away. It will be pointless for you to sow seed in your fields because your enemies will eat the harvest.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I will turn against you, and you'll be defeated by your enemies. People who hate you will rule over you, and you'll run away even when there's no one chasing you!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If after all this you still refuse to obey me, I will move to punishing you seven times over for your sins.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I will break your self-reliant strength you're so proud of, and make your sky hard as iron and your land hard as bronze.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Your strength will be completely wasted[^50] because your land won't produce crops, and your trees won't produce fruit.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If you go on opposing me and refusing to do what I tell you, I will make your punishments seven times worse, based on your sins.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will send wild animals to kill your children, wipe out your livestock, and make you so few in number that there won't be anyone on your roads.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: However, if in spite of all this correction you still don't change but go on opposing me,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: then I will take action against you. I will punish you seven times over for your sins.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will send enemies with swords to attack you in retaliation for breaking the agreement. Even though you retreat into your towns for defense, I will plague you with disease, and you will be handed over to your enemies.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will send a famine so bread is in short supply. One oven will serve the needs of ten women baking bread. It will be distributed by weight so that you'll eat but won't have enough.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: However, if in spite of all this you don't obey me, but go on opposing me,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: then I will take action against you in a rage of fury, and I will punish you myself seven times over for your sins.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: You will eat the bodies of your own sons and daughters.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will destroy your high places[^51] of worship, smash your altars of incense, and pile up your dead bodies on what remains of your idols, which also have no life at all. I will really despise you.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will demolish your towns and destroy your pagan sanctuaries, and I will refuse to accept your sacrifices.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will devastate your land myself, so that your enemies who come and live in it will be horrified at what has happened.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I'm going to scatter you among the nations. You will be chased out by armies with swords as your land is ruined and your towns are destroyed.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: At least then the land will be able to enjoy its Sabbaths all the time it's abandoned while you are in exile in the land of your enemies. The land will finally be able to rest and enjoy its Sabbaths.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The whole time it's abandoned the land will observe the Sabbaths of rest that it wasn't able to do while you were living there. [^52]
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: I will make those of you who survive so discouraged that as you live in the lands of your enemies even the sound of a leaf blowing in the wind will scare you into running away! You'll run away like you're being chased by someone with a sword, and fall down even though no one is after you.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: You'll trip over each other as you run away from the attack, even though no one's there. You'll have no power to resist your enemies.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: You'll die in exile and you'll be buried in a foreign country.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Those of you who do manage to survive in the countries of your enemies will wither away because of their guilt, decaying as they share the sins of their fathers.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: They need to confess their sins and those of their fathers, acting in such an unfaithful way towards me, opposing me.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Because of this I took action against them and exiled them in the countries of their enemies. However, if they humbly give up their stubborn attitude and accept their punishment for their sins,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: then I will fulfill the agreement I made with Jacob, Isaac, and Abraham, and I will not forget my promise about the land.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: For the land will be left empty by them, and it will enjoy its Sabbaths being abandoned. They will pay for their sins, because they rejected my rules and regulations.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: But despite all this, even while they are living in land of their enemies, I will not reject or hate them so much as to destroy them and break my agreement with them, because I am the Lord their God.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Because of them I will renew the agreement I made with their fathers, those I led out of Egypt as the other nations watched, in order that I might be their God. I am the Lord.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: These are the rules, regulations, and laws that the Lord set up between himself and the Israelites through Moses on Mount Sinai.
